Results & Figures

Quick Start - Format

  • Bio-reps: append _1, _2… to the siRNA name (e.g. siGENE_1, siGENE_2).
  • Tech-reps: assign the same siRNA name and Primer to multiple wells — CTs are averaged automatically.

Results & Figures

All ▼
2⁻ΔΔCt Method (Livak & Schmittgen, 2001)

Step 1 – Average technical duplicates (2 wells → 1 bio-rep):
mean_Ct = (Ct_rep1 + Ct_rep2) / 2
Step 2 – ΔCt per bio-rep:
ΔCt = mean_Ct(target) − mean_Ct(reference gene)
Step 3 – NTC baseline ΔCt (pool all NTC bio-reps per primer):
mean_ΔCt_NTC = arithmetic mean of all NTC ΔCt values for that primer
Step 4 – ΔΔCt per bio-rep:
ΔΔCt = ΔCt(siRNA) − mean_ΔCt_NTC
Step 5 – Relative expression per bio-rep:
Rel. Exp. = 2^(−ΔΔCt)
Step 6 – Geometric mean across bio-reps & KD%:
Mean Rel. Exp. = 2^(mean of log₂(Rel. Exp.)) = 2^(−mean(ΔΔCt))
KD% = (1 − Mean Rel. Exp.) × 100
SE = SD(Rel. Exp.) / √n  [linear scale, around geometric mean]

Geometric mean is used for fold-change data (Rel. Exp.) because 2^x is convex — arithmetic mean would overestimate the center (Jensen's inequality). For siNTC: mean(ΔΔCt) = 0 by definition, so Mean Rel. Exp. = 2⁰ = exactly 1.0 (100%).
